meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Analysts say Gaza 'civilian' deaths include Hamas, other terror members working as medics, media workers
items.summary: Researchers say Hamas and PIJ martyr lists reveal Gaza terrorists who posed as doctors, nurses and journalists, clouding civilian casualty figures.

items.title: Churchill, Shakespeare and the UK flag all under siege in modern Britain, commentators say
items.summary: Britain's statues, flags and icons like Churchill and Shakespeare are under attack, as commentators warn DEI ideology is erasing the nation's history.

items.title: Dad loses custody of autistic son after fighting sex change, gets support from Elon Musk
items.summary: Father in Iceland says he lost custody after opposing his 11-year-old autistic son's sex reassignment, claiming courts prioritized ideology over parental rights.

items.title: Trump proven right on Iran's long-range missile capability as regime targets US-UK base, experts say
items.summary: Experts say Iran's Diego Garcia missile launch exposes deception about range limits, and analysts warn Europe now faces a direct ballistic threat.

items.title: Iranian man, Romanian woman charged after allegedly trying to enter UK nuclear missile base, officials say
items.summary: An Iranian man and Romanian woman have been charged after allegedly trying to enter HM Naval Base Clyde, the U.K.'s nuclear submarine base in Scotland.

items.title: Families of Iran's elite live lavishly abroad while ordinary citizens suffer at home
items.summary: Critics say children of Iran's ruling elite live freely in the West while their families enforce strict rules at home, with calls to sanction them.

items.title: Live possum discovered hiding among plush toys in an Australian airport gift shop
items.summary: A wild possum found a clever hiding spot among plush toys in an airport gift shop in Australia and was safely removed, staff confirmed.

items.title: Australian prime minister heckled at mosque, called 'putrid dog' by protestors
items.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese faced a hostile reception at Sydney’s Lakemba Mosque as protesters label him a "genocide supporter" over the Gaza conflict.

items.title: Iranian man, 2nd person arrested after allegedly trying to enter UK nuclear missile base: report
items.summary: Authorities in the United Kingdom told Fox News Digital that two people were arrested after unsuccessfully attempting to enter HM Naval Base Clyde in Scotland.

items.title: Ukraine peace talks on ‘situational pause’ as Middle East conflict intensifies: Kremlin
items.summary: The Kremlin says Ukraine peace talks are on a "situational pause" as Zelenskyy signals a Ukrainian team could resume negotiations as soon as Saturday.

items.title: Denmark secretly prepared to blow up Greenland's runways to stop US aircraft: report
items.summary: Denmark disguised a troop deployment to Greenland as a NATO exercise called Arctic Endurance, with soldiers carrying explosives, DR reported, citing 12 high-level sources.

items.title: Iran’s new supreme leader linked to properties with ‘line of sight’ into Israeli UK Embassy
items.summary: Two London apartments linked to Mojtaba Khamenei sit less than 50 meters from the Israeli Embassy which a terrorism specialist says constitutes a serious security breach.

items.title: Iran arrests 97 people it accuses of being 'soldiers of Israel' in massive crackdown
items.summary: Iran has detained hundreds since the war began, including 97 accused of ties to Israel, following the assassination of intelligence minister Esmaeil Khatib in a targeted Israeli strike.

items.title: Trump rates Macron 'an 8' as France and US split over Middle East strategy
items.summary: Macron calls for Middle East de-escalation while Trump and Israel push military pressure against Iran, exposing growing diplomatic rifts over regional strategy.

items.title: Neither the US nor Israel will 'succeed in replacing the Iranian regime,' retired US general says
items.summary: Retired U.S. Lt. Gen. Mark Schwartz told an Israeli newspaper that he believes "neither Israel nor the U.S. will fully succeed in replacing the Iranian regime."

items.title: 12 Arab and Islamic countries unite to condemn 'heinous' Iranian attacks
items.summary: A coalition of 12 Arab and Islamic countries denounces Iran’s attacks on civilian infrastructure, urges restraint and warns of risks to regional security.

items.title: Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei 'misfunctioning,' not controlling regime: sources
items.summary: Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, took power after his father Ali Khamenei was killed in an Israeli strike, but Israeli intelligence says he lacks control over the regime.

items.title: Iran’s hidden mountain nuclear site raises urgent threat, must be ‘neutralized': reports
items.summary: Andrea Stricker warns the U.S. must neutralize Iran's Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site and secure uranium stockpiles before ending combat operations.

items.title: Russia ships fuel to Cuba using 'spoofing' tactic challenging Trump embargo: reports
items.summary: Hong Kong-flagged tanker allegedly delivered 190,000 barrels of Russian oil to Cuba using AIS spoofing and ship-to-ship transfers to evade U.S. sanctions.

items.title: Venezuela's Delcy Rodriguez replaces sanctioned loyalist defense minister with military intel head
items.summary: Acting president Delcy Rodriguez has appointed Gustavo Gonzalez Lopez to lead the defense ministry, a move to consolidate military control.

items.title: One dead after cable car detaches, plummets at Swiss ski resort
items.summary: A 61-year-old woman died Wednesday after a gondola detached and crashed on a snowy mountainside in Engelberg, Switzerland, authorities said.

items.title: Former Assad-era prison chief convicted of torture in US federal court, marking a historic first
items.summary: Samir Ousman Alsheikh, a former Syrian prison chief, was found guilty of torture and immigration fraud charges, becoming the first Assad-era official convicted in a U.S. court.

items.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of killing hundreds in Kabul hospital strike
items.summary: Reported airstrike on Afghanistan hospital allegedly kills 400+ civilians, sparking widespread criticism over muted international response to the attack.

items.title: Belgium deploys military to guard Jewish sites after Iran-linked group claims Europe attacks
items.summary: Belgium deploys military to protect Jewish communities after explosion outside Liège, Belgium, synagogue. Officials call blast a "despicable antisemitic" act.

items.title: Fiery aftermath of Iran missile strike near Tel Aviv caught on video after 2 killed
items.summary: Video footage captured fiery destruction and emergency crews responding after an Iranian missile strike near Tel Aviv, Israel, killed two people.
